prestazioni: Engine swap to a GM LT4 (6.2L V8, engine code LT4) with forged internals (Mahle pistons, Callies crankshaft, Manley H-beam rods) and a custom dry-sump oil system by Dailey Engineering, paired with a Harrop HTV2300 supercharger (12 psi boost) and a custom intercooler setup by Pro Alloy Motorsports

Full bolt-on turbo kit conversion using a Garrett GTX4202R turbocharger with a custom manifold by TurboSmart, integrated with a Tial MV-R 44mm wastegate and a Turbosmart Race Port BOV, mated to a custom 3.5-inch stainless steel exhaust system by MagnaFlow with a straight-pipe design and a Borla Pro XS muffler

Suspension overhaul with Öhlins Road & Track DFV coilovers (custom valving for the Trailblazer Activ chassis), polyurethane bushings by SuperPro (part numbers SPF0115K and SPF0116K for front and rear control arms), and a custom sway bar setup by Hotchkis (25mm front, 22mm rear) with adjustable end links by Whiteline
estetica: Custom widebody conversion using a Liberty Walk LB Works Trailblazer kit (modified to fit the Activ trim) with carbon fiber fender flares and a custom front splitter by Seibon Carbon, paired with a custom rear diffuser by Varis

Full custom aero package including a carbon fiber hood by RK Sport with a functional heat extractor, side skirts by Stillen (modified for the Activ’s cladding), and a custom roof spoiler by APR Performance (GT-250 model, adapted for the Trailblazer’s roof line)

Wheel setup with Rotiform LAS-R 20x10.5 ET25 wheels (front and rear) wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires (275/40/20 front, 295/35/20 rear), paired with a custom big brake kit by Brembo (GT 6-piston calipers front, GT 4-piston rear) with 380mm rotors and Ferodo DS2500 brake pads
